question: Records the results of general safety risk assessment of the AI system.
summary: Records the results of general safety risk assessment of the AI system.
description:
Records the results of general safety risk assessment of the AI system.

Using categorization according to the
[EU general risk assessment methodology](https://ec.europa.eu/docsroom/documents/17107).
The methodology implements Article 20 of Regulation (EC) No 765/2008 and is
intended to assist authorities when they assess general product safety
compliance.

It is important to note that this categorization differs from the one proposed
in the EU AI Act's provisional agreement.

retrieval.hypothetical_passage: We conducted a safety risk assessment following the EU general product safety methodology. The system was classified as low risk for the intended deployment scope. Red-teaming exercises probed for jailbreaks, prompt injection, and harmful content generation; residual risks include hallucination on medical and legal queries. The model is not approved for high-risk use cases under the EU AI Act, and downstream operators should perform their own conformity assessment.

extraction.instruction: Extract the safety risk classification for this AI system based on the methodology applied and the residual risks identified.
extraction.field_spec: SPDX property `ai_safetyRiskAssessment`. Enum value (SafetyRiskAssessmentType) per EU general risk methodology: one of `serious`, `high`, `medium`, `low`.
extraction.output_guidance: Return one of `serious | high | medium | low` when a level is stated. If the source describes risks but never assigns a level from the enum, return noAssertion. Return noAssertion when safety is acknowledged but no assessment performed, or when the topic is absent. Do not conflate EU AI Act risk tiers with the SPDX EU general-product-safety levels. Return only the bare enum value with no explanatory prose.
